prompt:
A 190 mm aluminum desoldering pump rests on an electronics bench, its cylindrical body and spring-loaded plunger clearly visible along with a small PTFE tip at the end. The plunger is cocked and ready, with a thumb button near the top indicating how it is triggered to suck up molten solder. A tinned PCB joint and a small puddle of solder on a nearby pad make it clear that the pump is used to pull solder away from PCB joints during rework.

The setting is a near-future solarpunk electronics station with soft, even lighting, a soldering iron stand and spool of solder close by, and organized bins of components in the blurred background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on the pump, and a composition that centers the desoldering pump and the PCB area it is pointed at while the rest of the workspace remains softly out of focus.
